# opnsense_ipsec_child (Resource)
Manages a ipsec child on OPNsense.
## Example Usage
```terraform
resource "opnsense_ipsec_child" "tunnel" {
  connection_id = opnsense_ipsec_connection.site2site.id
  local_ts      = "10.0.0.0/24"
  remote_ts     = "10.1.0.0/24"
  mode          = "tunnel"
}
```
<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `connection_id` (String) UUID of the parent IPsec connection.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description of the child SA.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this child SA is enabled. Defaults to `true`.
- `esp_proposals` (String) ESP proposals. Defaults to `default`.
- `local_ts` (String) Local traffic selectors.
- `mode` (String) IPsec mode: `tunnel`, `transport`, `pass`, or `drop`. Defaults to `tunnel`.
- `remote_ts` (String) Remote traffic selectors.
- `start_action` (String) Action on startup: `none`, `trap`, `start`, or `route`. Defaults to `start`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the IPsec child SA in OPNsense.
## Import
```shell
terraform import opnsense_ipsec_child.example 12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012
```
